data.title: JACK HENRY & ASSOCIATES COO Shanon G. McLachlan Sells Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameMclachlan Shanon G.
RelationshipCOO, 10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$36,508
SEC FilingForm 4

Shanon G. McLachlan, COO and 10% owner of JACK HENRY & ASSOCIATES, sold 227 shares of common stock on November 10, 2025, at a price of $160.83 per share, totaling $36,508. Following the transaction, McLachlan directly owns 1,420 shares of the company.

SEC Filing: JACK HENRY & ASSOCIATES INC [ JKHY ] - Form 4 - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: ESALQ Citrus Analysis
data.description: citrus analysis CITRUS/CEPEA: Low demand from Europe presses down orange prices in SP Cepea, 13 - Exporters had good perspectives for the 2025/26 season of orange juice, especially due to the exemption of the US tariff on imports of the commodity from Brazil and to the increase in the orange production in São Paulo state. However, the limited demand from Europe kept international sales of orange juice below-expected from July to October 2025. As for November, shipments may continue to register low performance, since European acquisitions are still moving at a slow pace. As a result, players from the crushing industry in São Paulo state have been cautious about purchasing fruits, keeping only contracts closed before and buying exclusively in the spot market - purchasers have been offering lower quotations, from BRL 35 to BRL 38.00 per box, delivered at the industry in SP. The impacts of the pressure from the industry have started to hit the market of in natura fruits. Due to low values paid by the processing industry, part of the production is expected to be allocated to the in natura market, which can increase the supply in the wholesale supply center and, consequently, press downquotations to producers. Between November 10 and 13, prices of the orange to the industry averaged BRL 39.95 per 40.8-kg box, downing 12.59% compared to that in the period before. According to data from Cepea, pear orange quotations in the in natura market moved down 2.76% this week compared to the previous, closing at BRL 58.61 per 40.8-kg box. Concerning lima oranges, prices averaged BRL 89.01 per box, 0.24% up. As for the tahiti lime, the average is at BRL 31.25 per 27.2-kg box this week, dropping 9.3% compared to the period before. Quotations of the tahiti lime to export have averaged BRL 35.87 per box, 11.23% down. For the Portuguese version please refer to or click on <ESQ/CIT/ANALYS>

data.title: VanEck Ethereum ETF SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
VanEck Ethereum ETF, a Delaware statutory trust designed to reflect the performance of ether (ETH) minus operating expenses, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights substantial growth in the Trust's Net Asset Value (NAV) driven by a significant increase in the price of ETH, alongside key operational updates.

Financial Highlights

Net Asset Value (NAV): The Trust's NAV reached $265,946,969, marking a 124.74% increase from June 30, 2025. This growth is primarily attributed to a 66.09% rise in the price of ETH.

Net Asset Value per Share: NAV per share increased by 66.00%, from $36.41 at June 30, 2025, to $60.44 at September 30, 2025, reflecting the upward movement in ETH prices.

Net Increase in Net Assets Resulting from Operations: The Trust reported a net increase of $86,212,750, driven by net unrealized appreciation on investment in ETH and net realized gains on ETH sold.

Net Investment Loss: The Trust incurred a net investment loss of $97,492, primarily due to the Sponsor Fee, with no other expenses reported during the quarter.

Business Highlights

Trust Overview: The VanEck Ethereum ETF is a passive investment vehicle that aims to track the performance of ETH, minus the operating expenses of the Trust. It does not engage in active investment strategies beyond this objective.

Share Transactions: During the quarter, the number of shares outstanding increased from 3,250,000 to 4,400,000. This net increase was due to the creation of 1,800,000 shares and the redemption of 650,000 shares, reflecting strong investor demand.

Net Asset Value (NAV) Performance: The Trust's NAV saw a significant increase of 124.74%, rising from $118,333,419 at June 30, 2025, to $265,946,969 at September 30, 2025. This was primarily driven by a 66.09% increase in the price of ETH.

ETH Price Movement: The price of ETH rose from $2,486 at June 30, 2025, to $4,129 at September 30, 2025, contributing to the increase in the Trust's NAV per share from $36.41 to $60.44.

Operational Expenses: The Trust's only ordinary expense during the period was the Sponsor's Fee, which was waived. The Trust's operations are primarily funded through the sale of ETH.

Future Outlook: The Trust does not anticipate any material changes to its liquidity needs. The Sponsor has agreed to assume most of the Trust's expenses, ensuring that the Trust's operations remain focused on tracking the price of ETH.

SEC Filing: VanEck Ethereum ETF [ ETHV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: VanEck Bitcoin ETF SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
The VanEck Bitcoin ETF, a Delaware statutory trust designed to reflect the performance of bitcoin minus operating expenses, has released its Form 10-Q for the quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report provides insights into the Trust's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and future outlooks.

Financial Highlights

While the report primarily focuses on operational metrics, it is noteworthy that the Trust's Net Asset Value (NAV) per share increased from $26.40 at the end of 2024 to $32.31 at the end of September 2025. This increase is primarily attributed to the rise in the price of bitcoin, which saw a 22.37% increase from $93,349 to $114,228 during the same period. Additionally, the Sponsor has waived the entire Sponsor Fee for the Trust's net assets up to $2.5 billion until January 10, 2026, resulting in no expenses for the Trust during the quarter.

Business Highlights

Trust Overview

The VanEck Bitcoin ETF is a passive investment vehicle that aims to track the performance of bitcoin. The Trust does not engage in any activities designed to profit from or mitigate losses due to changes in the price of bitcoin.

Share Transactions

During the quarter ended September 30, 2025, the Trust experienced an increase in the number of outstanding shares from 55,375,000 to 63,125,000. This change was due to the creation of 9,750,000 shares and the redemption of 2,000,000 shares.

Bitcoin Holdings

As of September 30, 2025, the Trust held 17,853.09 bitcoins, up from 13,716.83 bitcoins at the end of 2024. This increase was driven by the purchase of 6,179.64 bitcoins and the sale of 2,043.38 bitcoins over the nine-month period.

Net Asset Value (NAV) Calculation

The Trust's NAV is calculated based on the MarketVectorTM Bitcoin Benchmark Rate price at 4:00 p.m. EST. The NAV per share rose from $26.40 at the end of 2024 to $32.31 at the end of September 2025, largely due to the increase in the price of bitcoin.

Market Performance

The price of bitcoin increased by 22.37% from $93,349 at the end of 2024 to $114,228 at the end of September 2025. This significant rise contributed to the increase in the Trust's NAV.

Future Outlook

The Trust remains focused on tracking the price of bitcoin and does not anticipate engaging in any activities beyond this objective. The Sponsor has not identified any trends or events likely to materially change the Trust's liquidity needs.

SEC Filing: VanEck Bitcoin ETF [ HODL ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: PROVECTUS BIOPHARMACEUTICALS, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Provectus Biopharmaceuticals, Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing small molecule drugs for oncology, dermatology, and ophthalmology, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2024. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational progress, highlighting key developments in its clinical programs and business strategy.

Financial Highlights

  • Grant Revenue: $0, decreased by 100% compared to the same period in 2024 due to the completion and full recognition of grant revenue under the awarded program.
  • Total Operating Loss: $(1,275,607), increased by 33.5% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to a decrease in grant revenue and an increase in operating expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(1,323,629), increased by 30.5% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily as a result of decreased grant revenue and increased operating expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Common Share: $(0.00), consistent with the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Clinical Development Programs: Provectus Biopharmaceuticals is advancing its small molecule platform with drug candidates like PV-10 and PH-10 across oncology, dermatology, and ophthalmology. Key clinical trials include intratumoral PV-10 for melanoma and liver cancers, and topical PH-10 for psoriasis and atopic dermatitis.
  • Proof-of-Concept Programs: The company is conducting non-clinical studies for various indications, including pediatric solid tumor cancers, adult solid tumors, and blood cancers. These studies aim to establish proof-of-concept for different formulations and routes of administration.
  • New Product Launches: The company launched VisiRose, Inc., a clinical-stage start-up in collaboration with the University of Miami, to commercialize ocular research using PV-305.
  • RBS API and Drug Candidate Manufacturing: Provectus has developed a proprietary, patented process for synthesizing pharmaceutical-grade RBS, ensuring high purity and consistency for commercial pharmaceutical use. This process has been reviewed and accepted by multiple national drug regulatory agencies.
  • Business Strategy: The company aims to pursue drug approval pathways and co-development relationships for intratumoral PV-10, develop a systemically administered formulation of RBS for cancer treatment, and explore non-clinical activity for other disease areas.
  • Recent Developments: The company held its annual stockholder meeting, where stockholders authorized potential amendments to the company's Certificate of Incorporation for a reverse stock split and a decrease in authorized shares.
  • Future Outlook: Management plans to access capital resources through equity offerings, debt financings, and collaborations to fund ongoing operations and clinical trials. The company is focused on building into a commercial-stage biotechnology company.

SEC Filing: PROVECTUS BIOPHARMACEUTICALS, INC. [ PVCT ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: Sierra Leone, law firm Jenner & Block reach settlement over $8 million legal tab
data.description:
By David Thomas

The West African country of Sierra Leone has reached a settlement in principle with U.S. law firm Jenner & Block to resolve their fight over $8 million in allegedly unpaid legal fees.

U.S. Magistrate Judge G. Michael Harvey in Washington, D.C., disclosed the settlement in a brief order on Thursday, but gave no details. Attorneys for Sierra Leone and a spokesperson for Jenner did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

Chicago-based Jenner sued Sierra Leone in 2022 to recover fees the firm argued it was owed for defending the country in a dispute with iron ore contractor Gerald International Ltd over an export ban. Gerald International had sought more than $1.8 billion in damages in the case, which eventually settled.

Jenner said its work "turned out to be far more complex and voluminous than either party initially contemplated."

Sierra Leone paid Jenner $3.6 million in legal fees between 2019 and late 2021, the firm alleged, leaving a balance of $8.1 million.

Sierra Leone contested the lawsuit, arguing that Jenner had not shown it was due any more than the $3.6 million it was already paid. A federal judge in January rejected Sierra Leone's arguments that there was no valid contract between it and the firm and that the country was immune as a foreign sovereign.

data.title: Russian fighter crashes on training flight, two dead
data.description:
A Russian Su-30 fighter aircraft crashed on a training flight in the northwestern region of Karelia, killing the two-member crew, news agencies quoted the Russian Defence Ministry as saying.

The governor of Karelia region, Artur Parfenchikov, said on Telegram the aircraft crashed in a forest. There were no casualties on the ground.

Parfenchikov said an investigation into the cause of the crash was underway.

data.title: Nike shares gain after Wells Fargo turns bullish on improving visibility
data.description:
** Nike shares up 2.9% on Thurs and bucking the trend in the broader market after Wells Fargo turns bullish

** Wells Fargo upgraded NKE to 'overweight' from 'equal weight' and increased its PT on the stock to $75 from $60

** The median 12-mth PT on NKE is $84; the stock was last at $66.03

** Overall analyst recommendations on the stock include 23 "strong buy" or "buy" ratings, 15 "hold" ratings and 2 "sell" ratings, per LSEG

** Wells Fargo cited improving visibility for NKE, and said it sees the negative estimate revision cycle that Nike has been in for the past three years reversing in the next six to nine months

** "We believe for the first time in years a few things are happening," including that visibility is improving, "green shoots are appearing," and that headwinds to the business "are set to dissipate," Ike Boruchow and other Wells Fargo analysts write in the research note

** Nike "has the potential to exit FY26 growing revs +3-4% with GMs (gross margins) expanding +200bps," they add

** On September 30, Nike reported a surprise rise in Q1 rev and beat profit expectations as its turnaround effort gained traction

** NKE is up for a third session in a row but remains down about 13% YTD

** The S&P 500 was last down 1.5% on Thurs

data.title: SANMINA CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Sanmina Corporation, a leading provider of integrated manufacturing solutions and advanced technology components, has released its annual Form 10-K report for the fiscal year 2025. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the highly competitive electronics manufacturing services (EMS) industry.

Financial Highlights

Sanmina Corporation reported strong financial results for the fiscal year 2025:

  • Net Sales: $8,128 million, reflecting a 7.4% increase from the previous year, driven by new program wins and ramp-ups in communications networks and cloud infrastructure.
  • Gross Profit: $716 million, with a gross margin of 8.8%, up from 8.5% in the prior year, due to improved operating efficiencies and a favorable product mix.
  • Operating Income: $355 million, maintaining an operating margin of 4.4%, consistent with the previous year.
  • Net Income Attributable to Common Shareholders: $246 million, with a decrease in effective tax rate to 22% from 25% due to the release of tax reserves.
  • Diluted EPS: $4.46, reflecting the company's strong financial performance and efficient cost management.

Business Highlights

Sanmina's business performance in 2025 was marked by significant operational achievements:

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates two main business segments: Integrated Manufacturing Solutions (IMS) and Components, Products and Services (CPS). IMS, which includes printed circuit board assembly and test, high-level assembly and test, and direct order fulfillment, generated approximately 80% of total revenue. CPS, which includes advanced PCBs, backplanes, cable assemblies, and other components, accounted for about 20% of total revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: Sanmina operates in 20 countries across four continents, with a significant portion of its operations in lower-cost regions such as Latin America, Asia, and Eastern Europe. This geographical distribution supports the company's strategy to manufacture in cost-effective locations while meeting customer demands.
  • Sales Units: The company supports its customers with a workforce of approximately 39,000 employees, including 4,000 temporary employees, indicating a substantial operational scale to meet production demands.
  • New Product Launches: Sanmina has expanded its product offerings with advanced component technologies, including optical, RF, and microelectronics modules, as well as high-performance storage platforms for hyperscale and enterprise solutions.
  • New Production Launches: The company continues to invest in factory automation, process improvements, robotics, and artificial intelligence to enhance manufacturing efficiency and expand its production capabilities.
  • Future Outlook: Sanmina aims to capitalize on its comprehensive solutions to attract new customers and increase the number of manufacturing programs for which it provides multiple solutions. It plans to continue penetrating diverse end markets, focusing on industries with significant growth opportunities and complex, mission-critical products.

Strategic Initiatives

Sanmina Corporation has undertaken several strategic initiatives to strengthen its market position and drive long-term growth:

  • Acquisition of ZT Systems: Sanmina has strategically focused on expanding its presence in the Cloud and Artificial Intelligence ecosystem through the acquisition of ZT Systems for $1.6 billion. This acquisition aligns with the company's intent to enhance its capabilities in data center infrastructure manufacturing.
  • Capital Management: The company has actively managed its capital through various initiatives, including the repurchase of 1.4 million shares of its common stock for $114 million under its stock repurchase program. Sanmina also entered into a new credit agreement providing $3.5 billion in senior secured credit facilities to finance the ZT Systems acquisition and repay existing credit agreements. Additionally, the company has utilized a Receivables Purchase Agreement to manage liquidity by selling trade receivables.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Sanmina plans to continue leveraging its comprehensive product and service offerings to penetrate diverse end markets with significant growth opportunities. The company aims to maintain its focus on securing long-term customer partnerships and optimizing its product and portfolio mix towards higher value opportunities. Sanmina also intends to manage its liquidity effectively, ensuring sufficient resources to meet working capital requirements and support strategic growth initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

Sanmina faces several challenges and risks in its operations:

  • End Market and Operational Risks: Adverse changes in key end markets such as industrial, medical, defense, aerospace, automotive, and communications could reduce demand for Sanmina's products, impacting sales and profitability. Factors like intense competition, regulatory changes, and economic conditions such as inflation and high interest rates pose significant risks.
  • Supply Chain Disruptions: The company faces challenges from component shortages, extended lead times, and geopolitical tensions, which can disrupt supply chains and increase costs.
  • Customer Concentration: A substantial portion of sales comes from a small number of customers, making Sanmina vulnerable to order reductions or cancellations from these key clients.
  • International Operations: Sanmina's global operations expose it to risks such as changes in trade laws, political instability, currency fluctuations, and compliance with diverse regulatory environments.
  • Regulatory and Compliance Risks: The company must adhere to complex U.S. export controls and other regulations, particularly in its defense business, with non-compliance potentially resulting in fines and reduced revenue.
  • Cybersecurity Threats: Sanmina's reliance on IT systems makes it susceptible to cyberattacks, which could disrupt operations and lead to data breaches.
  • Environmental Regulations: Compliance with environmental laws is critical, with potential liabilities for hazardous waste management and site contamination.

Management acknowledges the impact of global economic conditions, including inflation and supply chain constraints, on operations. Strategies to mitigate these risks include diversifying the customer base, enhancing supply chain resilience, and investing in technology to improve efficiency and reduce costs.

SEC Filing: SANMINA CORP [ SANM ] - 10-K - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: PLATTS: 100--US crude stocks hit five-month highs as rising net imports offset strong refinery demand
data.description: (Platts)--13Nov2025/238 pm EST/1938 GMT **Commercial crude stocks climb 6.41 million barrels **Cushing stocks fall by 350,000 barrels **Gasoline, distillate stocks decline for sixth straight week A second straight week of significant builds pushed US crude oil inventories to five-month highs during the week ended Nov. 7, US Energy Information Administration data showed Nov. 13. US commercial crude stocks climbed 6.41 million barrels to 427.58 million barrels in the week ended Nov. 7, EIA data showed. The build exceeded recent market expectations after the American Petroleum Institute Nov. 12 reported US crude oil stocks rose 1.3 million barrels over the period. The build pushed US inventories to the highest outright level since late May, but left them still 4% below the five-year average for this time of year. Inventory builds were primarily confined to the US Gulf Coast, where stocks increased by 5.84 million barrels. Notably, inventories at the NYMEX delivery hub in Cushing, Oklahoma, decreased by 350,000 barrels to 22.52 million barrels. Refinery utilization climbed 3.4 percentage points to 89.4% of capacity. It was the largest weekly uptick since March 2024, and put refinery runs 2.6% above the five-year average. But the impact that this strong refinery demand had on inventories was offset by net imports that surged 55% to a five-week high 2.41 million b/d. Crude exports were the lowest since early September at 2.82 million b/d, while imports fell back to a two-week low of 5.22 million b/d. US gasoline and distillate inventories were both lower for a sixth straight week. Gasoline stocks dipped 950,000 barrels to 205.06 million barrels, while distillates declined 640,000 barrels to 110.91 million barrels. US Atlantic Coast gasoline stocks are now 8.3% below their five-year average following a 2.7 million barrel draw. US jet fuel stocks surged 1.12 million barrels to 42.82 million barrels, a three-week high and nearly 12% above normal for this time of year. The US federal government shutdown, which ended Nov. 13, contributed to staffing shortages that resulted in mounting flight cancellations in recent weeks. Implied demand for jet fuel was lower for a second straight week at 1.64 million b/d, but still 8% above the five-year average. Total product supplied for gasoline, the EIA's proxy for demand, climbed 1.7% week over week to 9.03 million b/d, the strongest since early August. Distillate appetite was around 8% stronger week over week at 4.02 million b/d. — Christopher Vanmoessner, christopher.vanmoessner@spglobal.com-- Edited by Valarie Jackson, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Gold falls 1% as broad market sell-off follows US government reopening
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump signs deal to end US government shutdown
  • Private surveys indicate job market weakness

By Anmol Choubey and Pablo Sinha

Gold prices fell 1% on Thursday, pulling back from a three-week high earlier in the session amid a broad market sell-off following the reopening of the U.S. government.

Spot gold lost 1.1% to $4,151.86 per ounce as of 02:16 p.m. EST (1916 GMT). Elsewhere, spot silver fell 2.3% to $52.18 after rising to its highest level since October 17 earlier in the session.

U.S. gold futures for December delivery settled 0.5% lower at $4,194.50.

The U.S. government will resume operations after a record 43-day shutdown, under an agreement that funds federal operations through January 30.

"Precious metals are caught in a widespread selloff, where stocks, bonds, the dollar, and crypto are all under pressure and in the red," said Tai Wong, an independent metals trader.

"It's a classic buy-the-rumor, sell-it-all after the U.S. government re-opens."

Earlier in the session, spot gold hit a session high of $4,244.94, the highest level since October 21.

Initially, gold and silver markets rallied on the expectation that economic data released after the end of the shutdown will reveal U.S. labor market weakness and push the Fed toward at least one December rate cut, said Jim Wyckoff, senior analyst at Kitco Metals.

However, citing worries about inflation and signs of relative stability in the labor market after two U.S. interest rate cuts this year, a growing number of Federal Reserve policymakers are signaling reticence on further easing.

Private surveys have indicated job market weakness.

While the U.S. central bank reduced rates last month, Fed Chair Jerome Powell cautioned that further easing this year was not guaranteed, partly due to a lack of data.

Lower interest rates typically benefit gold, which offers no yield and is often seen as a safe-haven asset during periods of economic uncertainty.

Platinum was down 2.8% at $1,569.65 and palladium fell 3.7% to $1,419.75.

data.title: Saudi to bolster trade with Lebanon after drug smuggling curbed
data.description:
Key points:
  • Saudi Arabia to ease controls on imports from Lebanon
  • Lebanese authorities praised for curbing drug smuggling
  • Hezbollah's disarmament remains a contentious issue

By Timour Azhari

Saudi Arabia plans imminently to boost commercial ties with Lebanon after Lebanese authorities demonstrated "efficacy" in curbing drug smuggling to the kingdom over the past months, a senior Saudi official said on Thursday.

Ties between Riyadh and Beirut have been strained for years due to the power of Iran-backed Hezbollah over Lebanese political and security affairs, but the kingdom sees an opening after the group was severely weakened by war with Israel last year.

Easing controls on imports, banned for years by Saudi Arabia, would be a first tangible sign of improvement, although Riyadh still wants to see the weak central government of Lebanon disarm Hezbollah - a demand Hezbollah has so far rejected.

"We will take imminent steps to bolster commercial relations between the two nations," said the Saudi official, who spoke on condition of anonymity.

"The Lebanese government and security forces have demonstrated efficacy in curbing drug exports over recent months."

A delegation from the kingdom will soon visit Lebanon for discussions "to resolve the barriers hindering Lebanese exports to the Kingdom," the source said, without elaborating on specific measures that might be taken or sectors that might be affected.

In 2021, Saudi Arabia banned all imports from Lebanon, citing smuggling of the methamphetamine-like drug Captagon, which was produced in Lebanon and Syria.

That put further pressure on Lebanon's crumbling economy, already crushed by a 2019 financial crisis, with agricultural exports in particular stranded from traditional Gulf Arab markets.

Western and Arab states have long said Syrian President Bashar al-Assad's government and Hezbollah were behind the illicit production and trade in the drug, and huge factories were found in government-held areas of Syria and the border region where Hezbollah had a strong presence after Assad was toppled.

Hezbollah has denied involvement. Assad's government collapsed under a rebel offensive in December 2024.

The senior Saudi official said Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, whose ascent to power was supported by Riyadh, had requested that the kingdom review its policy. The official said the Saudi government valued initiatives taken by the two leaders in the past months.

In September, Lebanon approved a landmark plan to disarm Hezbollah, starting south of the Litani River.

But implementation has been slow, due in part to logistical challenges and given fears that moving too quickly might instigate civil strife in a country with a long history of sectarian violence.

The United States has pressed the Lebanese government to move forward on the plan swiftly, with Israel saying Hezbollah was trying to rearm. Israel has continued to conduct near-daily strikes on what it says are Hezbollah targets, leaving many Lebanese feeling powerless and living in fear of the next attack.

"The new Lebanese administration's efforts to preclude the use of Lebanon as a platform for threats to Arab states’ security will yield advancements in bilateral relations," the senior Saudi official said.
